MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-01-2017 IN 206 -- APPEARANCES BY GENE V. HALAVANAU, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF AMY GOLDMAN; CHRISTOPHER C. COOKE, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS EDWARD HOLL, CARLO HOLL, MP MARITIME LLC, MERCHANT PARTNERS LLC, MP SEATRADE LP, MP MARITIME ANALYTICS CORPORATION -- DEFENDANTS EDWARD HOLL, CARLO HOLL, MP MARITIME LLC, MERCHANT PARTNERS LLC, MP SEATRADE LP, MP MARITIME ANALYTICS CORPORATION'S MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 6/26/17 IS CONTINUED TO 2/20/18 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THE DEADLINE FOR COMPLETING DISCOVERY AND FILING MOTIONS IS CHANGED, ACCORDINGLY, TO 8/28/2017. THIS ORDER DOES NOT EXTEND THE DEADLINE FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: JIROQUE; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EDWARD HOLL, CARLO HOLL, MP MARITIME LLC, MERCHANT PARTNERS LLC, MP SEATRADE LP AND MP MARITIME ANALYTICS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING THIS ACTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SATISFIED THEIR BURDEN OF PROVING THE EXISTENCE OF A VALID ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. (BRUNI V. DIDION (2008) 160 CAL. APP. 4TH 1272, 1282 (PARTY FILING A PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION BEARS BURDEN OF PROVING THE EXISTENCE OF AN ARBITRATION AGREEMENT)). (PLEASE SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS.) ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action
Off Calendar Jun-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gene Halavanau, Esq. (650-638-2370) of Halvanau Law Office for Plaintiff.
Chrisopher Cooke, Esq. (650-638-2370) of Murphy Cooke Kobrick LLP for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adpts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS EDWARD HOLL, CARLO HOLL, MP MARITIME LLC, MERCHANT PARTNERS LLC, MP SEATRADE LP and MP MARITIME ANALYTICS CORPORATION'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action is DENIED. Defendants have not satisfied their burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement. (Bruni v. Didion (2008) 160 Cal. App. 4th 1272, 1282 (party filing a petition to compel arbitration bears burden of proving the existence of an arbitration agreement)). (Please see word minutes for full details.) Order is signed in Open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/hk)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Civil
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Gene V. Halavanau of the law offices of Halavanau Law Office (415.692.5301) appears as counsel for plaintiff/party-in-oppostion

Attorney, Gregory Rougeau LLP of the law offices of Brunetti Rougeau LLP (415.992.8957) appears as moving counsel seeking to be relieved as counsel for defendant, Edward Holl

Edward Holl, Defendant/Non-oposing party appears in his own behalf.

The Court hears from both attorneys and defendant as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Plaintiff counsel's objections as to timing and as to corporate entity defendants are overruled.

Attorneys Kenneth A. Brunetti and Brunetti Rougeau LLPS's Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel of Record for DEFENDANTS EDWARD HOLL, CARLO HOLL, MP MARITIME LLC, MERCHANT PARTNERS LLC, MP SEATRADE LP, and MP MARITIME ANALYTICS CORPORATION is GRANTED. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. Defendant Edward Holl may make an ex parte application in Department 206 for continuance of trial date.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)
